response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior engineer, but their experience centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, sustained C++ expertise this role requires. Their only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and there is no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL practices, MQTT, message-broker architectures, or industrial automation domain experience. The systems-level and high-performance background (low-latency DLang services, concurrency) is transferable but insufficient for a role expecting 8–12 years of C++-focused, security-conscious industrial development.
strengths: Strong systems/performance background: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests per second
Broad engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
Long, varied career spanning embedded, desktop, web, and event-driven platforms
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago) versus the role's core C++/STL requirement
No exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No demonstrated MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA) experience
